title: Variation in blanchability in Virginia groundnut (Arachis hypogaea L.)
abstract:
Blanchability is ease of test/skin removal and cleaning of the groundnut (Arachis hypogaea L.) kernal
surgace.  It is atrait of economic importance in processed groundnut food products.  The blanching conditions
of pre-heating temperature of 110 degress celcius for 35 min, with 200 g kernal sample at a blanching time
of 2 min and blanching air pressure of 17.6 psi, which gave satisfactory results, were standardized.  Ten
Virginia bunch large-kernel groundnut varieties grown in 2007 rainy season and 32007/08 postrainy season
at the ICRISAT Centre were evaluated for their blanchability.
